title: Content-Length must be the byte count, not the string length
rule: When hand-building an HTTP request, set Content-Length to len(body.encode()) — the byte count of the encoded body — not the character/string length. Any multibyte UTF-8 (accents, emoji, CJK) makes bytes > chars, so a char-length Content-Length truncates the body server-side mid-character; on keep-alive connections the leftover bytes are parsed as the next request and desync the connection. Encode to bytes first, then take len().
triggers: JSON parse error only with non-ASCII
server hangs waiting for more body
